pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Cynewulf trilogy (Andreas, Elene, Juliana)
series_anchor.palette: weathered stone-grey, payne's grey shadow, raw umber, faded gold ochre, parchment cream
series_anchor.mood: Anglo-Saxon Christian devotion. Cold northern light. Sea-spray and stone. The interior of a candle-lit scriptorium at dusk.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Wet-on-wet pooling for the stone weight; dry-brush over the pooled wash to suggest weathered surface texture; gold-ochre touched in sparingly with a small brush, kept slightly lifted from full saturation.

work_metadata.title: Elene
work_metadata.author: Cynewulf
work_metadata.description: An Old English poem narrating the finding of the True Cross by Saint Helena, mother of Constantine the Great. A masterpiece of Anglo-Saxon religious narrative poetry combining history with spiritual devotion.

1_context.content_excerpt:
Two hundred winters had wound their way,
and three and forty told in number,
since the Wielder of Glory, the world's true Light,
was born among men to bring us solace.
Then Constantine ruled, the Caesar commanded,
helm of the Romans, a righteous ring-giver.
The lord of legions was loved by his people,
a shield to his warriors, strong in his reign.

Then word was brought to the war-leader’s ear
of a gathering horde, a grim invasion.
The Hunnish people and Hrethgoths hardened,
Franks and Hugas, a fearsome force,
had mustered their might by the river’s margin,
on Danube’s dark bank, a host of dar

output.reasoning: Elene centers on the discovery of the True Cross — an object of immense spiritual weight hidden beneath earth and time. A single iron nail, ancient and corroded, evokes the relic at the poem's heart without literalizing the narrative, and carries the cold northern devotion and stone-weight of the Cynewulf series.
output.subject: A single ancient iron nail, corroded and pitted, resting on a flat surface
output.palette: Payne's grey, raw umber, faded gold ochre
output.composition: The nail placed in the lower-left third of the painting, horizontal, angled slightly, with the pointed end trailing off into near-nothing as pigment dilutes toward the right edge; the upper two-thirds of the composition is open parchment cream negative space, breathing and still
output.watercolor_treatment: Heavy wet-on-wet pooling of payne's grey and raw umber at the nail's thickest section, pigment darkening in concentrated pools along the underside to suggest weight and corrosion; dry-brush scuffing dragged across the pooled wash while still damp to lift surface texture suggesting rust and pitting; gold ochre touched in with a fine brush along the upper ridge of the nail, barely saturated, lifted before it fully blooms; paper grain visible throughout the thinner washes; edges of the form bleed softly with no hard outline
output.prompt: A single ancient iron nail, corroded and pitted, rests horizontally in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, its pointed end dissolving into near-nothing as the pigment dilutes toward the right edge. Heavy wet-on-wet pooling of payne's grey and raw umber gathers darkest along the nail's underside, suggesting mass and age. Dry-brush strokes are dragged across the still-damp wash, lifting surface texture to evoke rust and weathered iron. A touch of faded gold ochre, barely saturated, is laid along the upper ridge with a fine brush and left to lift softly rather than bloom. Cold-press paper grain shows through the thinner washes. Edges bleed without hard outline. The upper two-thirds of the composition is open, silent, parchment-pale negative space.

output.reasoning: No significant issues. The prompt stays within the anchor palette and mood, specifies one concrete subject, and describes actual watercolor behavior with named pigments.

The critic recommends shipping the prompt as-is with a score of 9/10 and no issues listed. There are zero suggestions to evaluate. The original prompt is clean, honors the series anchor, specifies correct pigments and watercolor behaviors, and avoids all prohibited elements. The final prompt should be the original, unchanged.

Heavy wet-on-wet pooling of payne's grey and raw umber gathers darkest along the nail's underside, suggesting mass and age. Dry-brush strokes are dragged across the still-damp wash, lifting surface texture to evoke rust and weathered iron. A touch of faded gold ochre, barely saturated, is laid along the upper ridge with a fine brush and left to lift softly rather than bloom. Cold-press paper grain shows through the thinner washes. Edges bleed without hard outline. The upper two-thirds of the composition is open, silent, parchment-pale negative space.
output.changes_summary: No changes were made. The critic issued no issues and recommended shipping the prompt as-is at 9/10.
